stories.title: Monroe voters reject data center plan after energy and noise fears
stories.dek: The Monroe Times reports local opposition prevailed in blocking the proposed facility over concerns about power demand and industrial noise. Residents living within a few miles of the site say the project would have raised their utility bills without delivering meaningful tax relief. The decision keeps the county's zoning rules unchanged for now.
stories.source_title: Data Center a big ‘no’ - The Monroe Times

stories.title: Benson under fire for Big Tech cash while data centers multiply
stories.dek: The Michigan official has accepted donations from companies racing to build data centers across the state. Critics say the money undercuts any push to protect residents from higher power costs and land conflicts. The donations spotlight the growing political stakes around these projects.
stories.source_title: How Can Benson Stand Up to Big Tech When She Takes Their Money? - Michigan Enjoyer

stories.title: Lansing council votes six-month freeze on data centers
stories.dek: Lansing leaders approved a half-year pause on new data-center proposals to buy time for rules that protect residents. The move halts projects in the capital while officials weigh power, noise and tax impacts. It marks the latest city to hit the brakes on rapid expansion.
stories.source_title: Lansing City Council passes 6-month data center pause - Yahoo

stories.title: Haring Township crowd backs outright ban on data centers
stories.dek: Hundreds showed up in Haring Township to demand a full prohibition on data centers, fearing loss of rural quiet and higher utility bills. The strong turnout signals deep community concern over industrial-scale projects. Wexford County residents want to keep the area off-limits.
stories.source_title: Crowd comes out in support of banning data centers in Haring Township - Cadillac News

stories.title: Lowell Township residents face first data-center vote tonight
stories.dek: A project could arrive in Kent County with major power and land-use impacts. Neighbors want to know how much noise, traffic and tax revenue will actually reach them. The meeting decides whether the township opens the door or sets limits.
stories.source_title: Data center discussion in Lowell Township tonight - WZZM13.com

stories.title: Lyon Township delays data center vote, leaving residents waiting on power and noise impacts
stories.dek: The board postponed action on the project proposal. Oakland County residents near the site face uncertainty over added electricity demand and potential noise. The delay gives locals more time to organize before any final decision.
stories.source_title: Lyon Township postpones decision on data center project - The Oakland Press

stories.title: Warren may pause new data centers as grid strain concerns rise
stories.dek: City council is set to discuss a temporary moratorium. Macomb County residents could see limits on future projects that pull heavy power loads. The move would protect local infrastructure while rules are reviewed.
stories.source_title: Warren City Council could consider a temporary data center moratorium - The Detroit News

stories.title: Water table bounces back after tech campus dig drained neighborhood pond
stories.dek: A local pond that dropped sharply during work on the Oracle and OpenAI campus is now recovering. Residents downstream have long worried about wells and wetlands tied to the same aquifer. The episode shows how quickly large-scale excavation can affect everyday water sources in the township.
stories.source_title: Pond near Oracle, OpenAI data center construction site recovering after level drops - MLive.com

stories.title: Saline data-center campus now priced at $16 billion after financing closes
stories.dek: Developers have locked in funding that pushes the Saline Township project well above earlier estimates. The larger valuation means bigger future tax revenue but also heavier demands on power and roads. Neighbors are watching how the jump affects negotiations over abatements and infrastructure upgrades.
stories.source_title: Saline Township data center price tag balloons to $16B as developers lock in financing - AOL.com

stories.title: Saline Township to Decide on $147 Million Annual Tax Break for Oracle-OpenAI Data Center
stories.dek: Critics including Attorney General Dana Nessel question the need for property tax cuts on top of existing state exemptions, while the project is expected to create 450 permanent jobs.

stories.title: Bills target data center power and water use amid recalls and noise fights
stories.dek: Democratic senators introduced regulations on energy costs and water consumption. Local actions include Kent County recall petitions over a Microsoft proposal and a Dowagiac facility shifting from Bitcoin mining after noise complaints.

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.

meetings.topic: Vote on 12-year, 50% tax abatement for Oracle-OpenAI $43 billion hyperscale data center project
Special meeting to consider 50% property tax reduction for 12 years on Oracle-linked data center project
Informational session on University of Michigan/Los Alamos data center project updates, including history, legislative developments, and Q&A
Informational session on University of Michigan/Los Alamos proposed data center project, including updates on opposition and resource concerns
